summary: The q8 dataset contains 10 problems (q1-q10), each showing exactly 100 issues with no differentiating activity, traction, or proof-status metrics available. Because all counts are identical and uniform, the provided context offers no signal to distinguish which problems are progressing or solvable, suggesting the dataset may be aggregated, placeholder, or missing granular metadata.
problems: All 10 problems (q1-q10) report an identical flat count of 100 issues, providing no basis to rank activity or traction.
No proof-status breakdown (e.g., open vs. resolved, partial vs. complete) is present despite the 'Proof Status' heading, limiting solvability assessment.
The 'Issues Per Problem' section is empty, so per-problem issue categorization is unavailable.
Uniform metrics raise concern that the data is a placeholder or improperly populated rather than reflecting real research progress.
highlights: A complete and consistent problem set (q1-q10) is tracked, giving full coverage across the dataset.
Each problem has a substantial issue volume (100), indicating significant accumulated discussion or test cases to mine once categorized.
The dataset is well-structured for follow-up analysis once issue-type and proof-status fields are populated.

suggested_todos.title: Populate the empty 'Issues Per Problem' section with per-problem issue type breakdowns
Add proof-status granularity (open/partial/resolved) instead of raw issue counts
Investigate why all problems show an identical count of 100 issues to rule out a data export bug
Define traction/activity metrics (recent commits, contributor count, last-updated) to enable problem ranking
Re-run this insight report once richer metadata is available
Tag a subset of problems for manual review to bootstrap solvability estimates
